report: Good morning Will. You had an excellent night's sleep, logging just over seven and a half hours with a sleep score of 92, and your body woke up only once during the night. Your sleep structure was optimal with solid deep sleep at 19 percent and great REM at 28 percent. Your overnight HRV averaged 74, which is above your weekly average of 67, and your resting heart rate came in at a healthy 53 beats per minute. Your body battery recharged from 28 all the way up to 100 overnight, giving you a full tank to start the day. Yesterday you crushed your step goal with over 16,700 steps and burned 965 active calories, including a solid 49-minute run in Chicago where you covered about 6.2 kilometers at a moderate effort. Your training readiness sits at 60, reflecting good recovery but a moderately high recent training load from that big half marathon effort last week. Today would be a great day for an easy recovery walk or light mobility work to let your body consolidate those fitness gains.
